issue_title: "Failed to add user 'admin' ..." should not be an ERROR
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When using the environment variables `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN` and `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD` keycloak logs this on startup:

```
ERROR [org.keycloak.services] (main) KC-SERVICES0010: Failed to add user 'admin' to realm 'master': user with username exists
```

In other words: This error always appears except for the very first startup of keycloak. When deploying to kubernetes, there is no way to set these environment variables only for the first deployment.

We forward all `ERROR` logs to our 24/7, so this is annoying. I fail to see why this expected situation should be logged as an "error".

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Should be logged as an INFO.

### Actual behavior

Log-Level is ERROR.

### How to Reproduce?

Start a keycloak-container that uses the environment variables `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN` and `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD` while using a persistent data storage. Then stop and restart the container.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not display error log for initial admin creation

Closes #15789

Co-authored-by: Steve Weixel <steve.weixel@quantum.com>

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a861d633e2e6c3a6cd6ea8f062799247b0972d33/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java#L152-L161

This issue can be reproduced most of the time by starting a Keycloak, creating an admin/admin user, activating user profile in realm settings and activating the required action `Verify Profile`. Restart Keycloak and run the following script:

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
SERVER_URL=http://localhost:8080
KC_USER=admin
KC_PASS=admin
LOGINS=""
for i in {1..30}; do
LOGINS="$LOGINS
(curl -s --fail --show-error "${SERVER_URL}/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
-H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
-d "client_id=admin-cli" \
-d "username=${KC_USER}" \
-d "password=${KC_PASS}" \
-d 'grant_type=password' ) &"
done
eval $LOGINS
exit 0
```

issue_title: Realm localization: Inconsistent message resolving regarding language fallbacks for different themes
issue_body:
### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Currently two different approaches are taken regarding language fallbacks, when realm localization texts are specified.
This is difficult to understand for the users and should be unified.

Furthermore, in contrast to the message resolving of the properties files (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fc7c57ee12c61730bad089b190b5e2e51c24d3e2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/DefaultThemeManager.java#L224), none of these approaches currently considers the fallbacks for variants (e.g. "de-CH-1996" should have fallback "de-CH") and regions (e.g. "de-CH" should have fallback "de").

Currently there are the following approaches.
To keep the text short, I will use the abbreviations **S** (for **S**tatic i18n aka "theme properties") and **RL** (for **R**ealm **L**ocalization).

### Approach 1: "Realm-Localization-Default-Text with lower prio than static i18n"
The prio of messages is the following (highest prio first): **RL \<complete-language-tag>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language-region>, S \<language>, S \<default-language>, RL \<default-language>**

This approach is currently used in the following contexts (but doesn't work currently due to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15059):
- login-templates (FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider)
- email-templates (FreeMarkerEmailTemplateProvider)
- account templates (FreeMarkerAccountProvider)

It is pretty clear that this approach is not very helpful: The purpose of realm localization should be to customize localization, and this is not possible when _RL \<default-language>_ has a lower prio than _S \<default-language>_.
One example: In message_en.properties for our custom login theme, we have a default value of true for key theme.hideLocalizationDropdown. (We decide based on this value whether to hide the localization dropdown.) One of our customers changes this to false, via realm localization text for the default language en. (She wants that all users see the dropdown, regardless of their language.) Then users with language de will still see no dropdown, due to theme.hideLocalizationDropdown still being true, because _S \<default-language>_ has a higher priority than _RL \<default-language>_.

### Current Approach 2: "Realm-Localization-Default-Text with higher prio than static i18n"
The prio of messages is the following (highest prio first): **RL \<complete-language-tag>, RL \<default-language>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language-region>, S \<language>, S \<default-language>**

This approach works fine for the example above (theme.hideLocalizationDropdown): It is possible to overwrite the static messages for all languages with the realm localization text of the default locale. But it is questionable whether this is the expected behavior in most cases. The following things should be considered:
- In custom themes, it is possible to overwrite messages from the base theme. For symmetry reasons, it makes sense that realm localization behaves the same as a custom theme, with regards to overwriting message texts. The prio for custom themes is as follows (highest prio first, **SC** = **S**tatic **C**ustom theme, **SB** = **S**tatic **B**ase theme): **SC \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, SB \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>,  SC \<language-region>, SB \<language-region>, SC \<language>, SB \<language>, SC \<default-language>, SB \<default-language>**
- As a user, I would assume when I specify an English realm localization text for the Account UI title (there might be a typo in the Keycloak default text), this is NOT displayed in the German Account UI page - at least when everything is nicely translated to German. It might be confusing to have a completely German page, with an English title.

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

The priority of message resolving should be the same in all contexts. A common solution should be implemented that is
- well understood by users
- covers all common scenarios

We suggest to use the same approach that is used when overwriting messages in a sub theme.
The prio of messages would then be (highest prio first): **RL \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, S \<language>-\<region>-\<variant>, RL \<language-region>, S \<language-region>, RL \<language>, S \<language>, RL \<default-language>, S \<default-language>**

### Actual behavior

Mentioned in description.

### How to Reproduce?

Mentioned in description.

### Anything else?

Solving this issue would also fix https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15059.

We are happy to provide a PR for this - but wait for the maintainers' feedback regarding the suggested solution (see "Expected behavior")
commit_message:
Consistent message resolving regarding language fallbacks for all themes

- the prio of messages is now as follows for all themes (RL = realm localization, T = Theme i18n files): RL <variant> > T <variant> > RL <region> > T <region> > RL <language> > T <language> > RL en > T en
- centralize the message resolving logic in helper methods in LocaleUtil and use it for all themes, add unit tests in LocaleUtilTest
- add basic integration tests to check whether realm localization can be used in all supported contexts:
  - Account UI V2: org.keycloak.testsuite.ui.account2.InternationalizationTest
  - Login theme: LoginPageTest
  - Email theme: EmailTest
- deprecate the param useRealmDefaultLocaleFallback=true of endpoint /admin/realms/{realm}/localization/{locale}, because it does not resolve fallbacks as expected and is no longer used in admin-ui v2
- fix locale selection in DefaultLocaleSelectorProvider that a supported region (like "de-CH") will no longer selected instead of a supported language (like "de"), when just the language is requested, add corresponding unit tests
- improvements regarding message resolving in Admin UI V2:
  - add cypress test i18n_test.spec.ts, which checks the fallback implementation
  - log a warning instead of an error, when messages for some languages/namespaces cannot be loaded (the page will probably work with fallbacks in that case)

Closes #15845

issue_title: JPA Map Storage: Add transaction retry logic to LoginActionsService.authenticate
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Execution of performance tests for the new storage revealed that concurrent invocations of the `LoginActionsService` endpoint to authenticate users (path `/realms/{realm}/login-actions/authenticate`) occasionally leads to `RETRY_SERIALIZABLE` failures on CockroachDB.

We must employ the same logic used in `AuthorizationEndpoint` and `TokenEndpoint` to handle concurrent requests by running the code in a retriable transaction.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Concurrent execution of `LoginActionsService.authenticate` should finish with no transaction retry errors

### Actual behavior

Concurrent invocation of the `LoginActionsService.authenticate` endpoint lead to the `RETRY_SERIALIZABLE` errors on CockroachDB

### How to Reproduce?

Run the login performance tests on CRDB

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add retry logic to LoginActionsService#authenticate

In addition to that, avoid adding cookies on each retry.

Closes #15849

Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Incorrect Signature algorithms presented by Client Authenticator
issue_body:
### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

## Issue:

Presented signature algorithms for clients using client Client authenticator being "Signed JWT" or "Signed JWT with client Secret" are not correct

### Signed JWT client 

Doc states

```
Signed JWT is "Signed Json Web Token".

When choosing this credential type you will have to also generate a private key and certificate for the client in the tab Keys. The private key will be used to sign the JWT, while the certificate is used by the server to verify the signature.
```

Keycloak behaviour

1. Go to credentials
2. Select client authenticator "Signed Jwt"
3. Go to Signature algorithm

The proposed list of signature algorithm iss:
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
HS256
HS384
HS512
PS256
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

Client has his private key registered within a keystore. It means that asymmetric key algorithm are needs to be used.

The value HS256 HS384 HS512 are applying to HMAC which is a a symmetric algorithm. 

The client needs to use a asymmetric algorithm.

It is providing more confusion than anything else, and those values should be removed.

 

The list of proposed algorithms should be 
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

#### Signed JWT with client secret

The doc states

```
If you select this option, you can use a JWT signed by client secret instead of the private key.

The client secret will be used to sign the JWT by the client.
```

Keycloak behaviour:

1. Go to credentials
2. Select client authenticator "Signed Jwt with client credentials"
3. Go to Signature algorithm

The proposed list of signature algorithm proposes:
```
ES256
ES384
ES512
HS256
HS384
HS512
PS256
PS384
PS512
RS256
RS384
RS512
```

Here we are dealing with symmetric key encryption, where is no Keystore.

Even more, there is reference within the doc, or corresponding client authenticator panel to any JWKS URL.

Also the OIDC specification explicitly mention the HMAC based algorithm in the section for `client_secret_jwt` : https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#ClientAuthentication

Therefore the proposed list should only contains the symmetric algorithm: 
```
HS256
HS384
HS512
```

### Version

20.0.0
commit_message:
Incorrect Signature algorithms presented by Client Authenticator
closes #15853

Co-authored-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>

issue_title: The redirect URI cannot be verified during logout in the case when client was removed
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Assume scenario like this:
- Login user to some client `foo`
- Client `foo` is removed in the realm
- Now logout request is sent with `id_token_hint` from step 1 and `post_logout_redirect_uri` of client `foo`. Now the logout request should fail as it is not possible to verify redirect_uri. There should not be an attempt to verify redirect URI against clients of the realm (unless legacy switch is enabled)

### Version

20.0.1
commit_message:
The redirect URI cannot be verified during logout in the case when client was removed
closes #15866

issue_title: CVE-2022-41946 - Information Exposure vulnerability in org.postgresql:postgresql
issue_body:
### Description

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: org.postgresql:postgresql
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-jdbc-postgresql@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-jdbc-postgresql@2.13.5.Final) › org.postgresql:postgresql@42.5.0

# Overview

[org.postgresql:postgresql](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.postgresql/postgresql) is a Java JDBC 4.2 (JRE 8+) driver for PostgreSQL database.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure in the pgjdbc driver, which writes to the operating system's shared temp directory when the InputStream to either `PreparedStatement.setText(int, InputStream)` or `PreparedStatemet.setBytea(int, InputStream)` is larger than 2K. The temporary file is readable by other users. This is the default system behavior on Unix systems but not on MacOS.

**NOTE:** This vulnerability is only fixed for JDK 1.7. Systems using JDK 1.6 or below can work around the vulnerability by setting the environment variable `java.io.tmpdir` to a non-world-readable location.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.postgresql:postgresql` to version 42.2.27, 42.3.8, 42.4.3, 42.5.1 or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/commit/34855cffb9079edf95cf77930f3e3b73f6a1b139)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/commit/9008dc9aade6dbfe4efafcd6872ebc55f4699cf5)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pgjdbc/pgjdbc/commit/91b8c2ca600f82d2ec47c3fb54ca2036b000e320)

issue_title: [CVE-2022-4147] Access Restriction Bypass vulnerability in io.quarkus:quarkus-vertx-http
issue_body:
### Description

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: io.quarkus:quarkus-vertx-http
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx-http@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx-http@2.13.5.Final)

# Overview

[io.quarkus:quarkus-vertx-http](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus) is a Cloud Native, (Linux) Container First framework for writing Java applications.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass via the `CORS` filter which allows simple `GET` and `POST` requests with invalid Origin to proceed.

# Remediation

Upgrade `io.quarkus:quarkus-vertx-http` to version 2.14.2 or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/commit/cc26704d3bed46ed1d5caee90f22894952a07182)
-   [RedHat Bugzilla Bug](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2148867)

issue_title: Keycloak Export only accept H2 datase-URL (Datasource: URL format error; must be jdbc:h2 ... but is jdbc:mariadb: ...)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

I like to **export Keycloak-data from a MariaDB database**, but it's not possible.

The source I use is: https://www.keycloak.org/server/importExport

The export command `export` **only accepts a H2 database-URL**, even I've created a build with proper database vendor. I've created a new build before execute the export: `build --db=mariadb`

There is an error displayed if I try to export the Keycloak-data from the MariaDB database. 

The error is:

`Datasource '<default>': URL format error; must be "jdbc:h2:{ {.|mem:}[name] | [file:]fileName | {tcp|ssl}:[//]server[:port][,server2[:port]]/name }[;key=value...]" but is "jdbc:mariadb://db.my-domain.de:3306/keycloak" `

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

The expected behavior is , that the `export` command accept a MariaDB database-URL like `jdbc:mariadb:...`.

### Actual behavior

The actual behavior is , that the `export` command only accept a H2 database-URL like `jdbc:h2:...` even if I execute `build --db=mariadb` before.

Also, the `show-config` commands shows that `kc.db =  mariadb (KcEnvVarConfigSource)` it isn't.

### How to Reproduce?

```
version: '3.9'

services:
  keycloak-export:
      container_name: keycloak-export
      image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
      environment:
        KC_HOSTNAME: login.my-domain.com
        KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT_HTTPS: true 
        KC_HTTP_ENABLED: false
        KC_PROXY: edge
        KC_DB: mariadb
        KC_DB_URL_HOST: db.my-domain.com
        KC_DB_URL_DATABASE: keycloak
        KC_DB_URL_PORT: 3306
        KC_DB_USERNAME: ***
        KC_DB_PASSWORD: ***
        KC_HTTPS_KEY_STORE_FILE: /keystore.p12
        KC_HTTPS_KEY_STORE_PASSWORD: ***
      command:
        - export --dir /export_data
      volumes:
        - ./keystore.p12:/keystore.p12
        - ./export_data:/export_data
      restart: "no"
```


### Anything else?

In production mode, the Keycloak-Server connects to MariaDB-Database-Server based on this Docker Compose file:

```
version: '3.9'

services:
  keycloak:
      container_name: keycloak
      image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
      environment:
        KC_HOSTNAME: login.my-domain.de
        KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT_HTTPS: true 
        KC_HTTP_ENABLED: false
        KC_PROXY: edge
        KC_DB: mariadb
        KC_DB_URL_HOST: db.my-domain.de
        KC_DB_URL_DATABASE: keycloak
        KC_DB_URL_PORT: 3306
        KC_DB_USERNAME: ***
        KC_DB_PASSWORD: ***
        KC_HTTPS_KEY_STORE_FILE: /keystore.p12
        KC_HTTPS_KEY_STORE_PASSWORD: ***
        KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: ***
        KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: ***
        KC_TRANSACTION_XA_ENABLED: true
        KC_HEALTH_ENABLED: true
        KC_METRICS_ENABLED: true
      command:
        - start
      ports:
        - 8080:8080
      volumes:
        - ./keystore.p12:/keystore.p12
      restart: always

```

The connection is successfully and Keycloak-data is stored in MariaDB database.

### Stackoverflow discussion
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/74718428/keycloak-export-only-accept-jdbch2-url-datasource-url-format-error-must-be-j

issue_title: CVE-2022-42889 - Apache Commons Text prior to 1.10.0 allows RCE
issue_body:
### Description

# Description
* Package Manager: maven
* Vulnerable module: org.liquibase:liquibase-core
* Introduced through repackaging of commons-text transitive dependency as part of org.liquibase:liquibase-core

# Detailed paths
* `liquibase.version` defined as 4.16.1 in pom.xml

# Overview

Repackaged transitive dependency org.apache.commons:commons-text included by org.liquibase:liquibase-core is vulnerable to CVE-2022-42889.

> Apache Commons Text performs variable interpolation, allowing properties to be dynamically evaluated and expanded. The standard format for interpolation is "${prefix:name}", where "prefix" is used to locate an instance of org.apache.commons.text.lookup.StringLookup that performs the interpolation. Starting with version 1.5 and continuing through 1.9, the set of default Lookup instances included interpolators that could result in arbitrary code execution or contact with remote servers. These lookups are: - "script" - execute expressions using the JVM script execution engine (javax.script) - "dns" - resolve dns records - "url" - load values from urls, including from remote servers Applications using the interpolation defaults in the affected versions may be vulnerable to remote code execution or unintentional contact with remote servers if untrusted configuration values are used. 


# Remediation

Upgrade `org.liquibase:liquibase-core` to version 4.17.1 or higher.

# References

[NVD Entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-42889)
[Keycloak Defined Liquibase Version](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/pom.xml#L135)
[Liquibase Release Including Fixed Dependency](https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/releases/tag/v4.17.1)
[Liquibase Commit Addressing Dependency](https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/commit/47a17396ee5e3382dfbfe7e8930887f52e0541a5)
commit_message:
Exclude commons-text from liquibase-core dependency

Closes #15915

issue_title: API call to get user profile config should allow any admin role.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The REST call to GET the user profile config, `admin/realms/my-realm/users/profile` currently requires the `view-realm` role.  This causes a bug if a user has, for instance, `query-users` role combined with fine grained access.  The user profile config is used by the admin UI to dynamically create the user form in the admin console.  (It will soon also be used in account console)

There is no security problem with a user reading the user profile config as it does not provide any user data.  Therefore, the profile config should have `auth.requireAnyAdminRole()` instead of `auth.realm().reqireViewRealm()`.


### Version

main

### Expected behavior

UI can acquire the user profile config with any admin role. 

### Actual behavior

UI can only get user profile config with `view-realm` role.

### How to Reproduce?

See description above.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CVE-2022-41854/CVE-2022-38752 Snakeyaml vulnerable to Stack overflow leading to denial of service
issue_body:
### Description

Overview

[org.yaml:snakeyaml](https://code.google.com/p/snakeyaml/source/browse/) is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java.

Affected versions ( < 1.32) of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to missing nested depth limitation for collections.

Keycloak is currently using Liquibase version 4.16.1, which is affected by the vunerability.

Details

Those using Snakeyaml to parse untrusted YAML files may be vulnerable to Denial of Service attacks (DOS). If the parser is running on user supplied input, an attacker may supply content that causes the parser to crash by stack overflow. This effect may support a denial of service attack.

Remediation

Upgrade org.yaml:snakeyaml to version 1.32 or higher.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15089. In Keycloak the dependency is coming from org.liquibase:liquibase-core. This dependency has to be upgraded to version 4.17.0 or higher.

issue_title: [CVE-2022-41881] Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in io.netty:netty-codec-haproxy
issue_body:
### Description

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final) › io.netty:[netty-codec-haproxy@4.1.82.Final](mailto:netty-codec-haproxy@4.1.82.Final)

# Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to a stack overflow that can be triggered by a malformed message containing a deeply nested TLV. (The only limitation on such recursion is that the TLV length cannot exceed `0xffff`.)

**NOTE:** The StackOverflowError is caught if HAProxyMessageDecoder is used as part of Netty’s ChannelPipeline.

# Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

# Remediation

Upgrade io.netty:netty-codec-haproxy to version 4.1.86.Final or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/netty/netty/commit/cd91cf3c99123bd1e53fd6a1de0e3d1922f05bb2)
- [CVE-2022-41881](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2022-41881)
- https://github.com/netty/netty/security/advisories/GHSA-fx2c-96vj-985v
- Related to #16120

issue_title: [CVE-2022-41915] HTTP Response Splitting vulnerability in io.netty:netty-codec
issue_body:
### Description

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: io.netty:netty-codec
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-netty@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-netty@2.13.5.Final) › io.netty:[netty-codec@4.1.82.Final](mailto:netty-codec@4.1.82.Final)

# Overview

[io.netty:netty-codec](https://github.com/netty/netty) is an event-driven asynchronous network application framework.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to HTTP Response Splitting when calling `DefaultHttpHeaders.set` on an iterator of values, because header value validation is not performed.

# Workaround

This vulnerability can be worked around by changing the `DefaultHttpHeaders.set(CharSequence, Iterator<?>)` call into a `remove()` call, and calling `add()` in a loop over the iterator.

# Remediation

Upgrade `io.netty:netty-codec` to version 4.1.86.Final or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/netty/netty/commit/fe18adff1c2b333acb135ab779a3b9ba3295a1c4)
- [CVE-2022-41915](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2022-41915)

issue_title: SSSD User Federation dissapeared in 20.0.1/20.0.2
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

The SSSD User Federation feature, described here (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_sssd), seems to have been removed in the latest versions of keycloak.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/15587#discussioncomment-4267475.

Was this an accidental removal, or is it intended that this feature be removed. If intentional, I suggest that the documentation be updated.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

When sssd is configured on the keycloak server's host machine, SSSD User Federation should be listed as an option in the User Federation admin page, and it should be possible to use the functionality.

### Actual behavior

The SSSD User Federation option never shows up in the admin page.

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the instructions at https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_sssd. The SSSD User Federation never shows up.


### Anything else?

Happy to help debug the issue. I'm on libera in the freeipa channel.

issue_title: AccountConsole leaks translated messages into cached theme
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

With the new AccountConsole the the messages bundle containing translated keys is reused when realm localizations are applied.
When the themes are cached, this results in the translated messages being written and cached into the translation bundle, and deletion of translation keys is no longer possible.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Deleted translation keys are respected and not shown anymore.

### Actual behavior

Deleted translation keys are not properly deleted from the cached theme instance.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a translation for a given key:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215426-4f22dd8a-4569-423b-85aa-fdb80c4d1521.png)
Check the translated key in the account console:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215520-54fd5d55-df25-4b9a-8495-f9e1042a1b8f.png)
Delete the translation:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215550-9b950fc1-9d19-435b-b6e9-d3194f2d2e27.png)
Check the key again, the translation still exists there.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215580-a90e0962-c61d-4151-b7d4-461e1e3f4065.png)

### Anything else?

The message bundle is loaded and, using `putAll`, updated with all localized texts.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a12f50f8d99b4eabada5c03a28832aee12098577/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/account/AccountConsole.java#L114-L118
After removing a key, the cached theme instance is not reset.

The theme instance comes from the `CachedThemeManager$ExtendingTheme` `getMessages` method, whenever the theme cache is enabled.

- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

For example running testsuite with this command on Java 17:

```
mvn -B -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/pom.xml clean install -Pauth-server-quarkus -Dtest=DefaultAuthzConfigAdapterTest
```
the test fails. The reason is, that class `java.lang.System` is not available in the JSP files, which are used by authorization adapter tests. This is failing when `app-server-undertow` is used for adapters testing (which is the default).

There are multiple authorization adapter tests affected by this issue. The whole stacktrace:

issue_title: operator doesn't watch other namespaces
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

according to [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12105) and [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/11938#issuecomment-1125201574) the operator should be watching namespaces defined in the `QUARKUS_OPERATOR_SDK_NAMESPACES` env variable, but it doesn't seem to work for me.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

reconcile keycloak resources in other namespaces than the operator runs in.

### Actual behavior

reconciles only in the operator's namespace

### How to Reproduce?

deploy operator with:
```
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    app.quarkus.io/build-timestamp: 2022-12-20 - 19:05:31 +0000
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "2"
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: keycloak-operator
    meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: keycloak-operator
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
    app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak-operator
    app.kubernetes.io/version: 20.0.2
  name: keycloak-operator
  namespace: keycloak-operator
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak-operator
      app.kubernetes.io/version: 20.0.2
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        app.quarkus.io/build-timestamp: 2022-12-20 - 19:05:31 +0000
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: keycloak-operator
        app.kubernetes.io/version: 20.0.2
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: OPERATOR_KEYCLOAK_IMAGE
          value: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.2
        - name: QUARKUS_OPERATOR_SDK_NAMESPACES
          value: iam-test
        image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak-operator:20.0.2
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 3
          httpGet:
            path: /q/health/live
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          periodSeconds: 30
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 10
        name: keycloak-operator
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 3
          httpGet:
            path: /q/health/ready
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          periodSeconds: 30
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 10
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: keycloak-operator
      serviceAccountName: keycloak-operator
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

```

create a keycloak resource in the iam-test namespace:
```
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
  annotations:
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: iam-test
    meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: iam-test
  creationTimestamp: "2023-01-03T12:54:17Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
  name: internal-test
  namespace: iam-test
  resourceVersion: "844051854"
  uid: ff5a05cb-8651-49fe-84fe-9ce437b0d6b4
spec:
  db:
    host: iam-db
    passwordSecret:
      key: password
      name: iam-db.credentials.postgresql.acid.zalan.do
    usernameSecret:
      key: username
      name: iam-db.credentials.postgresql.acid.zalan.do
    vendor: postgres
  hostname:
    hostname: login-internal-test.example.net
  http:
    httpEnabled: true
    httpPort: 8080
    httpsPort: 8443
    tlsSecret: keycloak-tls
  image: repo/keycloak-customized:20.0.2
  ingress:
    enabled: false
  instances: 1

```

### Anything else?

the operator's logs are also indicating that the watched namespace is only `keycloak-operator`:

issue_title: java.nio.charset.UnsupportedCharsetException: cp0 when running keycloak in nanoserver container
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [ ] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When trying to start keycloak using `kc.bat` only `start --optimized` works as expected.

All other command fail with `java.nio.charset.UnsupportedCharsetException: cp0`.

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

Expect all other commands to work as well.

### Actual behavior

Command fails with exception mentioned above.

### How to Reproduce?

Use one of the nanoserver based tags of https://hub.docker.com/_/eclipse-temurin.

Copy keycloak into container and build using` /keycloak/bin/kc.bat build`. 

Try to execute any 'interactive' command when executing the docker container.

### Anything else?

Appending `-Dsun.stdout.encoding=UTF-8 -Dsun.err.encoding=UTF-8` to `JAVA_OPTS_APPEND` solves this issue.
May be a good idea to append this to the standard arguments in kc.bat (since this file is probably only use on windows systems)

If needed I can create a small PR myself, to improve the batch script
commit_message: #16226 explicitly set UTF-8 as stdin and stdout encoding

issue_title: Wrong auth session id being used when validating auth session id cookies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When validating the auth session cookies to see if they have a corresponding valid authentication session on the server, the code is resolving the id from an iterator and potentially ignoring valid cookies when multiple authentication session cookies are set in the request.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/0f2ca3bfddeeeece06cae4cbd1f7122adb261eed/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationSessionManager.java#L205.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Should validate each cookie individually.

### Actual behavior

Only validates the first cookie set in the request, randomly.

### How to Reproduce?

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16132

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Wrong auth session id being used when validating auth session id cookies (#16253)

Closes #16252
Closes #16132

issue_title: Field generator: `getCollectionElementClass` method not generated when no addElement method is present in interface
issue_body: When there is no `addElement(E element)` method in entity interface, the `getCollectionElementClass` is not generated for the field in `Map*EntityFields`. It's caused by the fact that there is `FieldAccessorType.COLLECTION_ADD` used as a condition for adding it: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/10f2ae34113ec86fb80682b08fd4a27b68383db7/model/build-processor/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/processor/GenerateEntityImplementationsProcessor.java#L180-L185
commit_message:
Field generator: getCollectionElementClass method not generated when no addElement method is present in interface

Closes #16255

issue_title: Incorrect handling of OptionParserException in kcadm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

I ran a `kcadm` command (`config credentials [...]`) with an incorrect flag (`--username` vs `--user`). This raises `OptionParserException`, which results in a misleading error message.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

It should tell me that I put a bad flag or something instead.

### Actual behavior

This returns `Unknown command: credentials`, which is a misleading error.

### How to Reproduce?

Run something like: `/opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh config credentials --server http://localhost:8080 --realm master --username admin --password admin`

(this has the incorrect `--username` flag)

This will return `Unknown command: credentials`, which is a misleading/incorrect error message.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix: provide a better error message when option parsing fails (#27354)

closes: #16260

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>

issue_title: Do not show username field when updating profile if UPDATE_EMAIL feature is enabled and email as username is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When updating profile, the `username` field should not be available if:

* `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature is enabled, forcing users to update e-mail through a required action.
* `Edit username` is enabled and email as username is also enabled

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

The `username` field only visible when e-mail attribute is readable.

### Actual behavior

The `username` field is shown even though email as username is enabled and `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature is enabled.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a realm
* Enable User Profile
* Enable Registration
* Enable Email as Username
* Enable Edit Username
* Register a new user
* Enable the `Verify Profile` action to the newly created user
* Try to log-in as the user
* The `username` field is shown even though email as username is enabled

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not show username field when updating profile if UPDATE_EMAIL feature is enabled and email as username is enabled

Closes #16263
